slides.title: The compound your *gut* makes from pomegranate.
slides.sub: It is called urolithin A, and it may help your muscles age more slowly. Here is the science.

slides.title: Pomegranate, then bacteria
slides.body: Pomegranate is rich in ellagitannins. Your gut bacteria convert them into *urolithin A*, a compound your body cannot make on its own.

slides.title: It triggers mitophagy
slides.body: Urolithin A is linked to mitophagy: the cellular cleanup that *clears worn-out mitochondria* so fresh ones can take their place.

slides.title: Cleaner cellular engines
slides.body: Mitochondria are the power plants in every cell. Clearing the tired ones can support *steadier energy* as you age.

slides.title: Endurance you can feel
slides.body: Early trials suggest urolithin A *may help rebuild* muscle endurance and strength in older adults over a few months.

slides.title: Not everyone makes it
slides.body: Only some people host the bacteria that produce it. A diverse gut, fed by *whole plant foods*, gives you a better shot.

slides.title: Feed the habits behind *real energy*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the food, movement, steps and sleep that keep your body running well, all in one daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your gut can turn pomegranate into a compound that helps your muscles age more slowly. It is called urolithin A. 🌿🍷

It triggers mitophagy, the cleanup that clears worn-out mitochondria and may help rebuild muscle endurance. Feed a diverse gut with whole plant foods. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Urolithin A: The Gut Compound That Renews Your Mitochondria
